task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2225: Fix: Hex color normalization, gradient CSS, schema validation, and thumbnail rendering ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2225` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2225 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: Fix: Hex color normalization, gradient CSS, schema validation, and thumbnail rendering (#2225)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2225` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2225" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: Fix: Hex color normalization, gradient CSS, schema validation, and thumbnail rendering" body: "Closes #2225\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Add regex validation to the Zod schema for hex colors, accepting both formats (with/without `#`) to maintain backward compatibility with existing config data - Enforce templateId validation using `z.enum()` against the predefined `TEMPLATE_IDS` constant - Apply inline color normalization (`#` prefix check) directly in components rather than creating a shared utility, keeping changes scoped to the specified files - Replace synthetic CSS gradients with actual `thumbnailUrl` image rendering to display the intended template previews ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Schema Validation Hardening Strengthen data validation at the schema layer to ensure only valid hex colors and recognized template IDs are accepted, preventing invalid data from propagating through the system.
Task 1: Add Hex Color Validation to Presentation Template Schema Add regex-based validation to the colors array in the presentation template schema to ensure only valid 6-digit hex color strings are accepted. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-template.schema.ts` to define a `HexColorSchema` using `z.string().regex(/^#?[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/, "Expected a 6-digit hex color")` - Replace the current `z.array(z.string()).min(5).max(6)` colors definition with `z.array(HexColorSchema).min(5).max(6)` - The regex should accept colors both with and without `#` prefix to maintain compatibility with existing config data stored without the prefix
Task 2: Validate Template ID Against Allowed Values Restrict the templateId parameter in the save action to only accept values from the predefined `TEMPLATE_IDS` constant. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/generate/_actions/save-template.action.ts` to import `TEMPLATE_IDS` from the presentation template schema - Change `templateId: z.string().min(1)` to `templateId: z.enum(TEMPLATE_IDS)` in the validation schema - This ensures server-side validation rejects any template ID not in the curated list
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase strengthens data validation at the schema layer to ensure only valid hex colors and recognized template IDs are accepted. **Hex Color Validation:** - In `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-template.schema.ts`, create a `HexColorSchema` using `z.string().regex(/^#?[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/, "Expected a 6-digit hex color")` - Replace the existing colors array definition from `z.array(z.string()).min(5).max(6)` to `z.array(HexColorSchema).min(5).max(6)` - The regex accepts both formats (with and without `#` prefix) to maintain backward compatibility with existing config data **Template ID Validation:** - In `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/generate/_actions/save-template.action.ts`, import `TEMPLATE_IDS` from the presentation template schema - Update the validation schema by changing `templateId: z.string().min(1)` to `templateId: z.enum(TEMPLATE_IDS)` - This enforces server-side validation to reject any template ID not in the predefined list ```
### Phase 2: UI Color Normalization and Thumbnail Rendering Fix the visual rendering issues by ensuring hex colors include the required `#` prefix for CSS and replacing synthetic gradients with actual template thumbnail images.
Task 1: Fix Color Swatch Rendering Normalize hex colors before applying them to inline styles so browsers correctly interpret the color values. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/storyboard/_components/template-color-swatches.tsx` - Add inline normalization to the `backgroundColor` style: `color.startsWith("#") ? color : \`#${color}\`` - The `title` attribute already correctly adds the `#` prefix for display text
Task 2: Fix Gradient Preview and Fallback Correct the gradient CSS in the template picker by normalizing color values and using a valid CSS fallback instead of a Tailwind class name. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/storyboard/_components/template-picker.tsx` - Normalize hex colors in the gradient stops by ensuring `#` prefix (e.g., `#${templateData.colors[0]}` or using conditional check) - Replace the invalid `"bg-muted"` fallback with a valid CSS value such as `var(--muted)` or a hardcoded neutral color like `#e5e7eb` - Ensure the gradient syntax is valid: `linear-gradient(135deg, `#color1` 0%, `#color2` 100%)`
Task 3: Render Actual Template Thumbnails Replace synthetic gradient backgrounds with the actual template preview images using the `thumbnailUrl` field from template configuration. - Update `apps/web/app/home/(user)/library/_components/library-templates.tsx` - For template cards: replace the gradient `
` with an `` element using `template.thumbnailUrl` as the `src` - For the Sheet panel preview: similarly replace the synthetic gradient with the actual image - Add appropriate styling for the images (object-fit, aspect ratio) to maintain the current card layout - Include fallback handling (e.g., onError or conditional rendering) in case the image fails to load - Consider using Next.js `Image` component if the pattern is established elsewhere, otherwise standard `` is acceptable
